prompt_md:
**Context:** A team is mid-migration from an old data store to a new one. They are currently **dual-writing** to both stores, the old store is still authoritative, and reads still come from the old store. A new feature requires a column to be renamed. They have many app instances deployed, and they may need to roll back any deployment quickly.

An engineer proposes: "Let's just rename the column in place and drop the old name now — it's cleaner, and we'll fix any callers if they break."

Pick the best decision for this situation, **and** the reason that correctly justifies it.

decision_choices: Rename the column in place and drop the old name immediately, fixing callers as breakages appear
Stop the migration entirely until the rename is finished, then resume
Use expand–contract: add the new column alongside the old, dual-write and migrate readers, and drop the old column only after nothing references it
Switch all reads to the new store first to avoid dealing with the old column
reason_choices: Because keeping the old and new shapes compatible at every step preserves the ability to roll back any deployment safely
Because renaming in place is always faster and faster is always better during a migration
Because pausing all migration work is the only way to make any schema change
Because once reads move to the new store, backward compatibility no longer matters at all
